calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-03-2016 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. JUDGE JOHN K. STEWART; Clerk JLee. Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-22-2016 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-02-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-25-2016 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-04-2015 Continued To Jan-25-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Notice
Off Calendar Nov-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF IDA JACK-VEASEY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED IN PART as to Defendant City and County of San Francisco's affirmative defenses for assumption of the risk, comparative negligence and Proposition 51. It is undisputed that Defendant offers no facts, witnesses or documents supporting these affirmative defenses. (Response to Plaintiff's Separate Statement, p. 4.) Defendant's remaining affirmative defenses for failure to state a claim, immunity, and tort claims are not persuasively contested. Plaintiff does fail to state a claim: "common carrier" is an element of a negligence claim and not a separate cause of action. Governmental immunity likewise a proper affirmative defense and there is a triable issue of material fact as to Plaintiff's negligence claim under Code of Civil Procedure section 437(c), subd. (f). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
